[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 2]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   P = alpha * V * I
*   alpha = alpha_0 / (1 + I/I_s)
*   I_s = h * v / (sigma * tau)
*   v = c / lambda

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 2:**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Pressure [Torr]
        *   y-axis: Saturation intensity [kW/cm^2]
    *   Curves:
        *   T = 200 K
        *   T = 242 K
        *   T = 290 K

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 1:** Basic imaging setup for laser-induced fluorescence schemes
    *   Components:
        *   detector array
        *   to laboratory computer
        *   picture element (pixel)
        *   collection optics
        *   laser sheet
        *   collection volume
        *   laser-induced fluorescence

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   P: Absorbed power
*   V: Volume.
*   I: Laser intensity
*   alpha: Absorption coefficient
*   alpha_0: Unsaturated absorption coefficient
*   I_s: Saturation intensity
*   h: Planck's constant
*   v: Optical frequency
*   sigma: Absorption cross section
*   tau: Recovery time
*   c: speed of light
*   lambda: wavelength

title: iodine_floreence_hiller_and_hanson_1990
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   τ = 1 / (A21 + Q)
*   ηf = A21 / (A21 + Q)
*   αo = σ na
*   σ = h ν (B12/c) x g  for narrowband excitation
*
    σ = h ν (B12/c)x (1/Δνl)  for broadband excitation
*   na = n χa fp
*   ηc = (Ω/4π) * [1/(4f/#) * (M/(M+1))]^2
*   Pr = ηc ηf Pa
*   Pa = αo V Is; (Is >> I)
*   Pf = ηc h ν A21 V na

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   c:  speed of light in vacuum
*   λ:  wavelength in vacuum
*   τ:  recovery time
*   A21: Einstein coefficient of spontaneous emission (=fluorescence)
*   Q: quenching rate (removal of absorbed energy by collisions)
*   ηf: fluorescence efficiency
*   αo: unsaturated absorption coefficient
*   σ: absorption cross-section
*   na: number density of absorbing molecules
*   B21: Einstein coefficient of absorption
*   g: absorption line shape function
*  Δvl: laser spectral linewidth
*   n: total number density of molecules
*   χa: mole fraction of iodine molecules
*   fp: fractional population of iodine molecules
*   Pa: absorbed power
*   ηc:  collection efficiency
*   Ω: solid collection angle
*   f/#: f-number of the lens (ratio of focal length to lens diameter)
*   M: imaging magnification (ratio of image height to object height, or ratio of image distance to object distance)
    * Pr: fluorescent power
    * V: control volume
    * Is: laser power

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations**

*   None explicitly written, but there's an implicit relationship: `Seeding fraction is estimated from the ratio of the iodine vapor pressure at reservoir temperature (0.3 Torr, Fig. 3) and the mixing chamber pressure`. This implies a ratio calculation.

**2. Graphs/Charts**

*   **Fig. 3. Phase diagram of iodine**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Temperature \[°C] (0 to 200)
        *   y-axis: Vapor pressure \[Torr] (10^-1 to 10^3)
    *   Curves:
        *   Solid-Liquid phase boundary (Melting point at 1 atm)
        *   Solid-Gas phase boundary (Sublimation curve)
        *   Liquid-Gas phase boundary (Vapor pressure curve)
    *   Key Values:
        *   Triple point

*   **Fig. 4. Iodine seeding curves with allowed absorption (over 1 cm length) and gas flow temperature as parameters**
    *   Axes:
        *   x-axis: Pressure \[Torr] (0 to 375)
        *   y-axis (left): Maximum seeding fraction \[ppm] (10^2 to 10^5)
        *    y-axis (right): Mixing chamber pressure \[Torr] (10^2 to 10^3)
    *   Curves:
        *   5%, 290 K
        *   1%, 290 K
        *   1%, 200 K

**3. Tables**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters**

*   Baker 4-2208 (iodine crystals)
*   0.3 Torr (iodine vapor pressure at reservoir temperature)
*   <5 (Mach numbers)
*   10 (collisions)
*   1 cm (length)
*   750 Torr (mixing chamber pressure, atmospheric pressure)
*   400 ppm (iodine mole fraction)
*   200 K (temperature of gas flow)
*   232 Torr (pressure of gas flow)
*   1%/cm (absorption)
*   P13/R15 (43,0) B-X transition (excitation line center)

[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 7]
Ok, here is the technical content extracted from the image:

1.  **Mathematical equations:**
    *   fp = C1 * (2J + 1) * (theta_r / T) * (1 - e^(-theta_v / T)) * e^(-epsilon / kT)
    *   epsilon = Te + we * (v + 1/2) - wexe * (v + 1/2)^2 + Bv * J * (J + 1) - alpha_e * (v + 1/2) * J * (J+1)
    *   J = 0.83 * sqrt(T [K] / Bv [cm-1])

2.  **Graphs/charts:**
    *   **Top Graph:**
        *   X-axis: Wavelength [nm]
        *   Y-axis: Dissociation limit
        *   Curve: Iodine absorption spectrum, Progression V"=0 (values on the curve 0 to 40)
    *   **Middle Graph:**
        *   X-axis: cm^(-1) (range 19420 to 19432)
        *   Curve: Iodine absorption lines at 514.5 nm
    *   **Bottom Graph:**
        *   X-axis: Relative frequency [GHz] (range -12 to +8)
        *   Y-axis: no units on the graph
        *   Curves: Argon-ion gain profile (dashed line), Absorption spectrum

3.  **Tables:**
    *   Table 2: Line assignments
        *   Headers: (J', J"), Branch, (v', v"), FCF, Position [GHz]
        *   Data:
            *   (51, 50), R50, (44, 0), 0.01617, -9.11
            *   (61, 60), R60, (51, 1), 0.00416, -7.45
            *   (47, 48), P48, (44, 0), 0.01618, -4.42
            *   (102, 103), P103, (49, 0), 0.01167, -4.42
            *   (99, 98), R98, (58, 1), 0.00439, -0.47
            *   (12, 13), P13, (43, 0), 0.01779, +2.00
            *   (16, 15), R15, (43, 0), 0.01779, +2.00
            *   (87, 88), P88, (47, 0), 0.01290, +5.41
            *   (45, 46), P46, (50, 1), 0.00440, +5.41
            *   (29, 28), R28, (49, 1), 0.00470, +7.36
            *   (77, 78), P78, (46, 0), 0.01382, +8.90

4.  **Diagrams:**

title: iodine_floreence_hiller_and_hanson_1990
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 8]
Here's the technical content extracted from the document:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   B\_ij = 6 x 10^4. A\_ij [s-1] λ^3 [nm]
*   Q = sigma\_q,carrier * sqrt(8kT/π) * sqrt(1/m\_I2 + 1/m\_carrier)
*   g = (1 / sqrt(1 + I/Is)) * sqrt(4 ln 2 / π) * (1/ dv_i) * V(x,y)
*   x = 2 sqrt(ln 2)  dv / dv_i
*   y =  sqrt(ln 2) dv_h / dv_i * sqrt(1 + I/I_s)
*   dv = v - v\_r - dv\_p
*   dv\_p = -1.4 MHz * sqrt(P[Torr] / P0[Torr]) * sqrt(T[K]/298 / T0[K]/298)
*   dv\_h = 12 MHz * P[Torr] / sqrt(T[K]/298)
*   dv\_D = 210 GHz * (1/lambda[nm]) * sqrt(T[K]/m[amu])

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 3. Collision cross sections and quenching cross sections (room temperature) of iodine [Å²]**
    *   Headers: Gas, H2, He, N2, O2, Ar, CO2, Xe, I2
    *   Data:
        *   Quenching: 0.63, 0.79, 3.00, 3.24, 5.11, 7.40, 10.6, 65.0
        *   Collision: 4.6,  5.9,  5.5,  7.8

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Natural lifetime of 43-0 vibrational band: 0.44 us
*   B12 calculated value: 4 x 10^18 m^3 J^-1 s^-2.
*   Hyperfine splitting: 333.5 MHz

**3. Tables:**

*   Table 4: shows recovery time of 16 ns at 125 Torr

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Fluorescence power: 40 nW
*   Exposure time: 1 us
*   Number of photons: 120,000
*   Quantum efficiency of detector: 50%
*   Shot-noise limited signal-to-noise ratio (SNR): 240, better than 200
*   Pressure: 125 Torr, 750 Torr
*   Recovery time: 16 ns
*   Saturation intensity I = 70 kW/cm²
*   Factor of reduction of the line center value: factor of 3
*   Shot-noise-limited precision of SNR: better than 1%
*   Temperature Measurements: precision of 1%
*   Fletcher and McDaniel (1987): achieved an accuracy of 2%
*   Velocity Measurement: Error due to non-linear line shape/non-ideal behaviors is bout 5%
